prerequisites: A CIMIS (California Irrigation Management Information System) account. Weather data services require registration; register at the CIMIS website before requesting data.
A Web API AppKey issued to your registered account. Set this as the `api_key` config field.
A query window defined by `start_date` and `end_date` in yyyy-mm-dd format (both required).

One or more `targets` to query (the connector requires `targets` and `targets_type`). Per the CIMIS API, targets may be station numbers, California zip codes, decimal-degree coordinates, or street addresses, and target types cannot be mixed within one request.
Optionally, the daily and/or hourly data items to retrieve (`daily_data_items`, `hourly_data_items`) and a `unit_of_measure` (English or Metric).

authMethods.name: API Key (AppKey)
authMethods.summary: The CIMIS Web API authenticates with a per-account application key (AppKey). Provide it via the connector's `api_key` secret field. The connector passes it to the API as the `appKey` query parameter, which CIMIS requires on every weather-data request.

setupSteps.title: Register for a CIMIS account
setupSteps.body: Weather data services require registration. Create an account on the CIMIS website (et.water.ca.gov / cimis.water.ca.gov). Station and zip-code lookup services do not require registration, but the data this connector retrieves does.

setupSteps.title: Request your Web API AppKey
setupSteps.body: Log into your CIMIS account page, scroll to the end, and select the 'Get AppKey' button to obtain your unique Web API AppKey. CIMIS requires this key as a parameter on all data requests.

setupSteps.title: Provide the AppKey to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the issued AppKey in the `api_key` field. The connector sends it as the `appKey` query parameter when calling the CIMIS Web API.

setupSteps.title: Configure the query window and targets
setupSteps.body: Set `start_date` and `end_date` (both required, yyyy-mm-dd). Set `targets` and `targets_type` to the geographies or stations you want: per the CIMIS API these can be station numbers, California zip codes, decimal-degree coordinates, or street addresses, and the types cannot be mixed in one request.

setupSteps.title: Select data items and units (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set `daily_data_items` and/or `hourly_data_items` to choose which measurements to retrieve (e.g. temperature, precipitation, solar radiation, wind, relative humidity, evapotranspiration), and set `unit_of_measure` to English or Metric. If omitted, the API applies its defaults (English units).

sources.title: CIMIS Web API - FAQ (appKey required for data requests)
